items.title: Revamp Midjourney Shorten with GPT Completions in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the broken Discord upstream for the Midjourney shorten endpoint with a new implementation using OpenAI's chat completions. The public API contract remains unchanged, and a legacy Discord fallback is available as an opt-in option. Key improvements include enhanced error handling and a more reliable response format, ensuring seamless functionality moving forward.

items.title: Introducing Passkey Login and Management UI in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a complete Passkey/WebAuthn login and management UI, which is dark by default and flag-gated for controlled rollout. Key components include a new in-house WebAuthn shim, TypeScript models for passkeys, and user-friendly profile management features. The implementation ensures no impact on current users until the feature is enabled in production.

items.title: Enhance Passkey Support with New Models and Settings in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces foundational support for Passkey/WebAuthn in the AuthBackend, including two new models: Passkey and PasskeyChallenge. It also adds necessary settings and a migration for the new database tables, while ensuring no new HTTP endpoints are exposed at this stage. The changes are fully additive and reversible, setting the stage for future enhancements without impacting existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Passkey Support with New Endpoints in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the PasskeyViewSet and associated endpoints for WebAuthn, enhancing the backend's authentication capabilities. Key features include options for registration and authentication, along with management functionalities for user passkeys. The implementation is flag-gated, ensuring that the routes remain inactive in production until enabled.

items.title: Enhance Site Customization with New RFC in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `.plans/SITE-CUSTOMIZATION-V2.md` RFC, which expands on the previous SITES-CONSOLE framework by addressing user feedback on service visibility, pricing granularity, and homepage customization. Key changes include a streamlined data model with a new JSON column and additional tables, alongside a detailed implementation sequence for future development. This RFC sets the stage for improved site management and user experience.

items.title: Introducing Passkey/WebAuthn Design for Seamless SSO in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive design document for implementing Passkey/WebAuthn support in AceDataCloud SSO, located at `.plans/passkey.md`. Key features include a new RP ID for cross-subdomain functionality, backend library specifications, and the addition of two new tables for managing passkeys. The rollout will occur across six independent PRs, ensuring minimal disruption to existing services.

items.title: Rename 'Try Now' to 'Studio' for Clarity in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The top navigation menu item previously labeled 'Try Now' has been renamed to 'Studio' to better reflect its destination. This change affects the English and Chinese language files, ensuring consistency across the platform. The URL remains unchanged, maintaining a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance Site Branding at Boot in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a mechanism to load tenant-specific branding elements such as logos, favicons, and titles at application startup. Key changes include the addition of site state management, fetching site data on boot, and applying branding dynamically across the platform. This ensures that users see their custom branding immediately, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Improve Domain Binding Error Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the user experience by properly displaying error messages when attempting to bind a hostname that is already in use. A new helper function has been introduced to extract error messages from various backend response formats, ensuring users receive clear feedback. Additionally, new internationalization keys have been added to support multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance DNS Instructions for Custom Domains in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies how to enter 'Host record' for various DNS providers in the Custom Domain settings. A new hint paragraph has been added to guide users on entering subdomain prefixes, apex domains, and full FQDNs. The changes improve user experience by preventing common mistakes when configuring DNS records.

items.title: Enhance DNS Host Record Guidance in Frontend Web 🌐
items.summary: This update improves user experience by clarifying how to enter 'Host record' values across various DNS providers. A new hint has been added under the DNS record table, explaining the expected input formats for subdomains and root domains. The change is purely UX-focused, with no backend modifications, ensuring that existing functionalities remain intact.

items.title: Fix trailing slashes for site domain URLs in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a 404 error when unbinding custom domains by ensuring that the `get` and `delete` methods in the SiteDomain operator include a trailing slash in their URLs. The backend is already configured to handle slashed URLs correctly, so this change will improve functionality without requiring backend modifications. After deployment, users will see the expected behavior when using the Unbind button.

items.title: Enhance UserPicker to Show Added Users in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update modifies the UserPicker component to retain already-added users in the dropdown, marking them with a '已添加' label. The filtering logic has been adjusted to prevent misleading messages when searching for existing users. This change improves user experience by clearly indicating which users are already part of the admin list.

items.title: Enhance Subsite Domain Management in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two key enhancements to the 'My Subsites' settings tab in Nexior. First, bound custom domains are now displayed alongside the default subdomain, providing better visibility for operators. Second, the 'Open' button now triggers a confirmation dialog that allows users to select from all reachable URLs for the subsite, improving the user experience.

items.title: Fix overlapping description in Nexior settings 🛠️
items.summary: This update resolves a visual overlap issue in the Settings → Login Methods tab, where the description text for enabled login methods was overlapping with the toggle column. The fix involves adjusting the CSS for the layout, allowing the label column to grow and preventing it from pushing into the toggle area. The changes ensure a clean display at various dialog widths without affecting mobile layouts.

items.title: Enhance Default Auth Providers for New Sites in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enables email, Google, GitHub, and phone authentication methods by default for new sites, while keeping WeChat disabled. Existing sites will automatically adopt these new settings during their next initialization without requiring manual updates. The change streamlines the onboarding process for new users and enhances the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance User Feedback for Non-Existent Users in Nexior ⚠️
items.summary: This update improves the user experience in the UserPicker component by displaying a warning message when a searched user is not found. A sentinel row is added to show '未找到该用户' when the API returns an empty response, ensuring users receive clear feedback. The backend remains unchanged, maintaining strict privacy constraints.

items.title: Enhance Upload Experience with Progress Indicators in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two key fixes to improve the user experience during image uploads. Users will now see a 0% progress overlay while files are uploading, preventing premature clicks on the Generate button. Additionally, the Generate action is blocked until all uploads are complete, ensuring that requests are sent with valid image URLs.

items.title: Streamline User Query with Single Input in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the user query process on the admin support page by replacing three separate input fields (User ID, Email, Phone) with a single input field. The new functionality allows superusers to perform exact matches across multiple user identifiers, enhancing the user experience and resolving the previous 404 error. Additionally, internationalization updates have been made for better accessibility.

items.title: Enhance Admin User Lookup with Detailed Query in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new admin-only endpoint, `GET /users/query/?q=<keyword>`, allowing superusers to retrieve full user details. It replaces the previous 404 error for user lookups on the admin page, providing a more robust solution for user management. The frontend has also been updated to support this new functionality, ensuring seamless integration for admin users.

items.title: Enhance Admin Login Flow with ContinueAs Chooser in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the admin login experience by ensuring that the ContinueAs chooser is displayed even when admins log in without an active session. Key changes include refactoring the login logic to check for superuser status and redirect appropriately, enhancing the overall user experience. The implementation maintains existing behaviors for non-admin users and ensures no disruption in service.

items.title: Fix stray margin in task preview for OpenAI Image in Nexior 🎨
items.summary:
This update addresses a visual issue in the OpenAI Image studio task preview where a stray bottom margin was present on the last metadata row. By applying a CSS rule to reset the margin on the last rendered `

` element, the gap below the `任务 ID` is eliminated, ensuring a cleaner layout. The fix is effective across all branches of task status, maintaining consistency in the display.

items.title: Fix stray margin in task previews across all studios in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update applies a CSS fix to remove the stray bottom margin on the last metadata row in task previews for 15 studios, ensuring consistent rendering. The fix involves adding a specific rule to the existing styles for each studio's task preview component. This change enhances the visual consistency of the task previews, particularly when certain metadata fields are not displayed.

items.title: Streamline Admin Impersonation Logic in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `IMPERSONATION_ENABLED` environment variable, simplifying the admin impersonation process. Now, impersonation relies solely on the `is_superuser` check, ensuring that only authorized users can impersonate others while maintaining robust security measures. The changes enhance clarity and functionality without requiring any production environment adjustments.

items.title: Updated AI Q&A List: Kimi Takes the Stage in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces DeepSeek with Kimi in the public roadmap marketing string found in config/roadmap.json. The API capability for /deepseek/chat/completions remains intact, ensuring continuity in service. This change aligns with the maintainer's request to refine our offerings.

items.title: Streamline Default Fallbacks and FAQ in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes DeepSeek from the homepage FAQ for the Chinese version and replaces it with Kimi as the default fallback for Claude Code. The change enhances clarity in marketing materials while keeping DeepSeek models available for user selection. Additionally, minor modifications were made to the OpenClaw configuration to reflect this update.

items.title: Updated API Marketing Copy in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: The DeepSeek folder has been removed from the APIs marketing materials as per maintainer request. This includes the deletion of the DeepSeek README and integration guide, along with updates to the AI Chat README to remove references to DeepSeek. The /deepseek/chat/completions API endpoint remains intact, ensuring continued functionality.

items.title: Streamlined Docs Navigation by Hiding DeepSeek in Docs 📚
items.summary: This update removes DeepSeek from the public documentation navigation and intro copy as requested by the maintainer. The source guides and OpenAPI specifications for DeepSeek remain intact, ensuring that users can still access the necessary resources without clutter in the navigation. This change enhances the clarity and focus of the documentation for users.

items.title: Updated OpenAI Chat Blurb to Feature Kimi in O O B E Integration 🎉
items.summary: This update replaces mentions of DeepSeek with Kimi in the OpenAI chat capability blurb found in README.md and src/sap-register.ts. The functionality remains unchanged, as the /aichat capability continues to support DeepSeek models. This change enhances clarity in public documentation while maintaining existing capabilities.

items.title: Hide DeepSeek from Public View in Status Page 🚫
items.summary: This update introduces a filter to exclude the DeepSeek service from the public status page. The backend monitoring for DeepSeek remains unchanged, ensuring that service performance continues to be tracked. This change enhances the clarity of the public status board by only displaying relevant services.

items.title: Updated Skills Marketing Copy in Skills ✨
items.summary: DeepSeek has been temporarily removed from the public marketing copy across various documents, including AGENTS, README, and specific skill files. The API capability for /deepseek/chat/completions remains intact, ensuring no disruption in service. This change aligns with the maintainer's request for a streamlined presentation of available skills.

items.title: Updated organization profile by removing DeepSeek in .github 🚀
items.summary: The organization profile has been updated to remove DeepSeek from the public marketing copy on the homepage, as requested by the maintainer. The API capability for /deepseek/chat/completions and existing customer integrations remain intact, ensuring continuity for users.

items.title: Align OpenAI Task Filter with Updated Docs in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the OpenAI MCP task filter contract with the latest OpenAPI specifications. Key changes include the introduction of a constrained literal union for task types and the removal of outdated references. Additionally, user-facing documentation has been updated, and a new unit test ensures the correct handling of the 'images' type filter.

items.title: Enhance Auth Provider Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new helpers to ensure that the login, registration, and password reset pages respect per-site authentication provider settings from Nexior. The changes allow dynamic rendering of provider buttons based on site-specific configurations, improving user experience by accurately reflecting available login methods. The implementation ensures that any updates in the Nexior settings are immediately reflected in the UI.

items.title: Sync Confirmation: No Updates Needed for Overview in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update confirms that the `overview` section does not require any modifications as it is not included in the MCP service mappings. Verification showed that there are no related packages or files in this repository, making this PR a no-op sync acknowledgment. No changes were made to source, test, or configuration files.

items.title: Clarify OpenAI Tasks Polling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the OpenAI Tasks integration documentation by clarifying that the `task_id` is returned synchronously in callback mode, while `trace_id` is optional. The documentation now accurately reflects the recommended polling method and updates examples accordingly. Additionally, a migration ensures that the documentation is the authoritative source for translations across multiple locales.

items.title: Upgrade Capacitor to v8 and Node to 22 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully migrates the Capacitor toolchain from version 7 to 8, ensuring compatibility with Node version 22. Key changes include updates to the Android native project configurations, Gradle, and various dependencies, while iOS remains unchanged due to already meeting the minimum deployment target. The CI configurations have also been adjusted to reflect the new Node version, enhancing the overall stability and performance of the project.

items.title: Fix deploy permissions to restore production deploys in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This hotfix updates the permissions in the deploy.yaml file from 'contents: read' to 'contents: write', allowing the build-number action to create necessary git references. This change resolves the production deploy failures that occurred after the recent consolidation PR. The fix ensures that the deployment pipeline functions correctly, restoring the ability to push tags and deploy updates.

items.title: Streamline Docker Builds for Efficiency in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates Docker image builds into a single workflow, reducing redundant builds and saving approximately 1,200 Ubuntu minutes per month. The previous separate workflows for GHCR and Docker Hub have been merged, allowing for simultaneous pushes with improved caching. Additionally, several cleanups have been made to enhance security and performance.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for the i18n files, specifically from the zh-CN source. Key languages affected include Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese, with consistent modifications across all files. This enhancement ensures that the platform remains accessible and user-friendly for a diverse audience.

items.title: Enhance Site Management with Quota Display and Admin Controls in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two key features on the Console → Sites page. First, a sub-site quota indicator is now visible, showing users their current usage against the maximum allowed subsites. Second, administrators can now be managed directly from the Advanced tab, allowing for easy addition and removal of admin users with real-time updates.

items.title: New Login Methods Tab for Site Settings in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Login Methods' tab in the site settings, allowing admins to manage authentication providers displayed on the login screen. Site owners can now enable or disable providers like Email, Phone, Google, GitHub, and WeChat, as well as set a default login method. This enhancement improves user experience by providing a straightforward UI for managing login options.

items.title: Fix discount application for Global calls in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where users with ACE discounts were not seeing the expected price reductions on Global and Balance-funded calls. The fix involves updating the discount lookup to use the API's owning service, ensuring that discounts are correctly applied. Additionally, new tests have been added to verify the functionality and prevent regressions.

items.title: Enhance Order Creation with Affiliate Markup in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the affiliate Site's `markup_ratio` into the Order creation process, controlled by a new feature flag. The implementation introduces additional audit fields to `Order.metadata.pricing`, ensuring accurate tracking of pricing adjustments. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate the new functionality and maintain system integrity.

items.title: Optimize CI Workflows for Cost Efficiency in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces per-branch concurrency to the CI workflow, significantly reducing unnecessary check runs and expected savings of 300-500 Ubuntu minutes per month. Additionally, the paths-ignore settings have been expanded across multiple workflows to prevent unnecessary triggers, potentially saving another 200-400 minutes monthly. These changes aim to enhance efficiency and reduce costs associated with GitHub Actions usage.

items.title: Enhance security by blocking self-service redemption of free packages in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a security fix to prevent authenticated users from redeeming system-managed free packages through the orders endpoint. Key changes include adding validation to block any package with a price of zero that is system-managed, ensuring that only trusted server-side flows can grant these packages. Additionally, a migration marks the survey-reward package as private for future-proofing.

items.title: Streamline Site Creation Logic in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `parent_site_id` surface and refines the logic for enabling sub-site creation. The `canCreate` functionality is now based on the host, enhancing performance by eliminating redundant data fetches. Additionally, several localization keys related to the parent site have been removed, ensuring a cleaner interface.

items.title: Critical security fix for credential cap enforcement in Gateway 🔒
items.summary: This update addresses a critical security issue identified in an internal audit, ensuring that the credential `limited_amount` cap is enforced correctly on billable APIs. A missing negation in the code allowed users to exceed their set limits, potentially draining application balances. The fix includes a code modification and new regression tests to prevent future occurrences of this issue.

items.title: Streamline MCP Documentation into Two Hubs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates over 50 scattered Chinese articles into two main hubs for easier navigation: one for Claude AI and Desktop, and another for Claude Code. Key improvements include a hub-and-spoke structure, enhanced content organization, and the addition of cross-links to facilitate user access. This restructuring aims to improve user experience and accessibility of documentation.

items.title: Enhance site listing filter for console in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the console subsites page to use a backend-side filter based on the origin suffix instead of a client-side filter. This change resolves an issue where superuser-created subsites were not displayed due to the previous filtering method. The new approach ensures that all relevant subsites are listed correctly, improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance User Query Options in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new query options for users in the admin console, allowing searches by email or phone in addition to user ID. The Query User card now features three input fields, enhancing usability and accommodating support requests. All internationalization files have been updated to reflect these changes across 12 locales.

items.title: Enhance Subsite Listing with Origin Filter in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the subsite fetching logic to utilize a new backend filter, allowing for more accurate listings based on the origin suffix. By switching to the `origin__endswith` filter, we ensure that subsites created by superusers are no longer hidden, improving user experience. The change preserves existing functionalities while enhancing the data retrieval process.

items.title: Streamline Site Creation by Removing Redundant Metadata in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update eliminates the `metadata.parent_site_id` from the site creation process and proxy CNAME resolution, ensuring that the DNS hierarchy is the sole source of truth for parent relationships. Key changes include modifications to the `SiteListCreateViewSet` and `resolve_proxy_cname`, enhancing the reliability of subsite creation and quota enforcement. All tests have passed successfully, confirming the stability of the system post-implementation.

items.title: Optimize Cron Jobs for Efficiency in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the frequency of two hourly workflows, `auto-merge-ai-prs.yaml` and `auto-triage-issues.yaml`, to every 6 hours. This change is expected to decrease the monthly fire count from approximately 720 to around 120, saving an estimated 600 Ubuntu minutes. The workflows remain offset to avoid competition, ensuring that AI PRs and unassigned issues are still addressed promptly.

items.title: Optimize Cron Job Frequency for Efficiency in Status Page 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the cron schedule for `update_data.yml` from every 5 minutes to every 30 minutes, significantly reducing the number of workflow executions from 288 to 48 per day. This adjustment is expected to save approximately 1,600 Ubuntu minutes per month, optimizing resource usage while maintaining adequate data freshness for users. The ability to manually trigger updates during incidents remains intact.

items.title: Switch to Cloudflare Turnstile as Default Captcha in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the default captcha from hCaptcha to Cloudflare Turnstile for various user registration and sensitive operations. The implementation includes a simple toggle in the feature flags and ensures compatibility with existing links. No backend changes are required, and users can still opt for hCaptcha if needed.
